summary: Read and analyzed <machine-local-path> (1).md in read-only mode. Verified SHA-256 matches 4B8C7447D4048781935EC7D8B8223112953941BEB4060AA0661AD6D298AE4DE7, confirmed 1,285 lines, and extracted implementation constraints and top risks for installer, new-PC recovery, GitHub login restore, release artifact, bundled runtime, and multi-PC sync/conflict safety.
decisions: No source/workspace files were edited; this was a read-only analysis aside from the required DinoBrain task trace.

Treat the plan's core requirement as a mechanical contract: current release artifact plus GitHub login recovery and ongoing multi-PC sync safety must be proven by fixtures, manifest fields, status artifacts, and Korean UI/browser proof.
GitHub-only recovery means no separate recovery key, no manual clone, no manual global Node/Git/gh install, and no hidden token/config copy as the normal path.
next_steps: Implement or verify P1-12 after prerequisite authority/status gates so installer/runtime templates cannot reintroduce stale memory or overclaim authority.
Add release artifact manifest and runtime-staging tests that bind the same SHA/commit artifact to the authenticated and unauthenticated new-PC recovery fixtures.
Add two-PC divergent-history, dirty-tree, and conflict-classification sync fixtures before allowing final readiness to treat GitHub backup as safe.
